q.en: Why does Ermaline's mother make the dummy Harold?
q.zh: 埃尔玛琳的妈妈为什么要做假人哈罗德？

options.en: To make the car look like it has a man in it for safety during long drives
options.zh: 为了让车里看起来有个男人，长途开车时更安全

options.en: To use as a decoration for the house

explain.en: Mom says: 'When we ride in the car, we'll put him in the back seat so it looks like we have a man with us. That will make us safer, especially when we take those long drives for our summer vacation.'
explain.zh: 妈妈说：“我们坐车时就让他坐在后座，这样看起来车里有个男人。那样我们会更安全，尤其是夏天长途自驾游时。”

q.en: From whose point of view is the story told?
q.zh: 这个故事是从谁的视角讲述的？

explain.en: The narrator says 'I'd liked my name until I started kindergarten' and refers to 'my mom,' showing a first-person account by Ermaline.
explain.zh: 叙述者说“我从上幼儿园起就不喜欢我的名字了”，并称“我妈妈”，说明是埃尔玛琳的第一人称叙述。

q.en: What does Mom use to make the dummy look flesh-colored?
q.zh: 妈妈用什么让假人看起来有肉色？

explain.en: Mom says 'I'll use a pair of pantyhose!' and pulls and tugs the pantyhose over the wig stand.
explain.zh: 妈妈说“我用连裤袜！”，并把连裤袜拉扯着套在假人头上。

q.en: In the glossary, the word 'whittled' means—

explain.en: Glossary: 'whittled: carved with a knife (p. 10),' used when Mom 'whittled the nose out of Styrofoam.'
explain.zh: 词汇表：whittled 意为“用小刀雕刻（第10页）”，文中妈妈“用小刀把鼻子从泡沫塑料上刻出来”。

q.en: How does Harold's head stay attached to his body?
q.zh: 哈罗德的头是怎么固定在身体上的？

explain.en: 'I had to use a wooden cooking spoon to attach his head to his body,' Mom explained.
explain.zh: 妈妈解释说：“我不得不用一把木制汤勺把他的头固定在身体上。”

q.en: Why does the author include the detail that the doorbell 'barked and growled'?
q.zh: 作者为什么要写门铃“会汪汪叫、低吼”这个细节？

options.en: To show the family had a dangerous dog
options.zh: 为了说明家里有只危险的狗

options.en: To show Mom's extreme safety measures and the story's humor
options.zh: 为了表现妈妈极端的安保措施以及故事的幽默

explain.en: The book says Mom 'had hooked the doorbell switch to a tape recording of a dog' as part of being 'a certified safety fanatic.'
explain.zh: 书里说妈妈“把门铃开关连到一段狗叫的录音上”，这是她“公认的安保狂”表现之一。

q.en: What happens to the thief who breaks into the car?
q.zh: 闯进车里的小偷发生了什么？

options.en: He is startled by Harold, falls, and knocks himself out
options.zh: 他被哈罗德吓了一跳，摔倒并昏了过去

explain.en: Officer McDuff says 'the guy uncovered your dummy and was so startled that he fell and knocked himself out on the pavement.'
explain.zh: 麦克达夫警官说：“那家伙掀开了假人，被吓了一大跳，摔倒在人行道上昏了过去。”

q.en: At the end, what award does Harold receive?
q.zh: 结尾时，哈罗德获得了什么奖项？

explain.en: The mayor 'put a ribbon with a medal that said "Good Citizenship Award" around Harold's neck.'
explain.zh: 市长“把一条系着奖章的绶带挂在哈罗德脖子上，奖章上写着‘优秀公民奖’。”

q.en: Which word best describes Ermaline's feeling about riding with Harold?
q.zh: 哪个词最能形容埃尔玛琳对和哈罗德一起坐车的感受？

explain.en: Ermaline says she would 'totally die of embarrassment,' and the glossary defines humiliated as 'completely embarrassed (p. 20).'
explain.zh: 埃尔玛琳说自己会“尴尬得彻底没脸见人”，词汇表把 humiliated 定义为“彻底尴尬（第20页）”。

q.en: What lesson does the story teach through the dummy's unexpected heroism?
q.zh: 通过假人出人意料的英雄行为，这个故事传达了什么道理？

options.en: Over-the-top safety ideas can sometimes work out surprisingly well
options.zh: 过于夸张的安保点子有时也会出奇地奏效

explain.en: Although Ermaline expected to be 'totally humiliated,' the dummy ends up catching a thief and earning a Good Citizenship Award—Mom's odd safety idea succeeds.
explain.zh: 虽然埃尔玛琳本以为会“彻底丢脸”，但假人最终抓住了小偷并获得了优秀公民奖——妈妈那个奇怪的安保点子成功了。

prompt.en: Design your own 'safety dummy' for a funny problem at home or school. Draw it and label the materials you would use.
prompt.zh: 为你家里或学校里一个好笑的问题设计一个属于自己的“安全假人”。画出来并标注你会用到的材料。

en: Pick one everyday worry (e.g., a dark room or an empty seat).
List 3 household items you would use to build your dummy.
zh: 选一个日常小烦恼（比如漆黑的房间或空座位）。
列出你会用来制作假人的3种家居用品。
